auto_applied_changes_log.change: Updated campaign website (gregmillerforjustice.com -> millerforjustice.org, now live), refreshed endorsements (still none, site has no endorsement page), fundraising ($0 PDC), one_liner, deep_read, signals, and expanded_signals based on direct review of millerforjustice.org. Lean held at 'unclear' / Low — site is deliberately policy-free, no Article VII or Culliton content. Keene v. Edie noted as stare decisis signal (cuts both ways).

Updated endorsements field with primary-source confirmed list from odonnellforjustice.com/endorsements (Gregoire, Smith, Strickland, Upthegrove, Harrell, Satterberg, Robnett, Brown + bipartisan slate).
Refreshed 2026 cash contribution totals via data.wa.gov PDC open-data (kv7h-kjye) using scripts/refresh_pdc.py; updated raised + as_of for 13 candidates with filer_id.

Added Democratic LD organization endorsements: 32nd District Democrats slate endorsements (Melody Pos1; Diaz/Hawk dual Pos3; Birk Pos4; Angelis Pos5; Stephens Pos7) and 31st LD Democrats endorsements (Diaz Pos3; Birk Pos4) with primary URLs.

notes.note: Endorsement list now primary-confirmed: mainstream-Dem with bipartisan municipal/LE reach. Tells us about his coalition, not about how he reads Article VII. Lean remains unclear/Low.

progress_log.note: Campaign announced expanded endorsements list (names not visible in LinkedIn post). Potential signal but insufficient for lean resolution.
